Skip navigation links
  • Overview
  • Package
  • Class
  • Tree
  • Deprecated
  • Index
  • Help
SEARCH

Deprecated API

Contents

  • Terminally Deprecated
  • Classes
  • Methods
  • Enum Constants
  • Terminally Deprecated Elements
    Element
    Description
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.command.flags.RemovePlayerFlags
    The --remove-players flag is being removed in favor of a more flexible system that allows for specifying a destination for players to be teleported to when a world is unloaded. See RemovePlayerDestinationFlags.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.config.node.ConfigNode.Builder.onSetValue(BiConsumer<T, T>)
    Use ConfigNode.Builder.onLoadAndChange(NodeChangeCallback) instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.config.node.ValueNode.getSerializer()
    Use ValueNode.serialize(Object) and ValueNode.deserialize(Object) instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.config.node.ValueNode.onSetValue(T, T)
    Use ValueNode.onLoadAndChange(CommandSender, Object, Object) instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.destination.Destination.getDestinationInstance(String)
    Use Destination.getDestinationInstance(CommandSender, String) instead. This method will no longer be called by DestinationsProvider.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.event.world.MVWorldClonedEvent.getFromWorld()
    Cloning can be done from unloaded worlds as well. Use MVWorldClonedEvent.getSourceWorld() instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.module.MultiverseModule.registerEvents(Class<? extends Listener>)
    Use MultiverseModule.registerDynamicListeners(Class) with the new DynamicListener API.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.MultiverseCore.getApi()
    Use MultiverseCoreApi.get() directly.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.teleportation.AsyncSafetyTeleporterAction.teleport(Entity)
    Use AsyncSafetyTeleporterAction.teleportSingle(Entity) instead, as teleport of single entity may result in multiple teleports due to vehicle and passengers based on AsyncSafetyTeleporterAction.passengerMode(PassengerMode).
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.ReflectHelper.getClass(String)
    Use ReflectHelper.tryGetClass(String) instead, which returns a Try that can be used to handle the failure case more explicitly.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.ReflectHelper.getField(C, String)
    Use ReflectHelper.tryGetField(Class, String) instead, which returns a Try that can be used to handle the failure case more explicitly.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.ReflectHelper.getField(Class<C>, String)
    Use ReflectHelper.tryGetField(Class, String) instead, which returns a Try that can be used to handle the failure case more explicitly.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.ReflectHelper.getFieldValue(C, String, Class<V>)
    Use ReflectHelper.tryGetField(Class, String) then map to ReflectHelper.tryGetFieldValue(Object, Field, Class) instead, which returns a Try that can be used to handle the failure case more explicitly.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.ReflectHelper.getFieldValue(C, Field, Class<V>)
    Use ReflectHelper.tryGetFieldValue(Object, Field, Class) instead, which returns a Try that can be used to handle the failure case more explicitly.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.ReflectHelper.getMethod(C, String, Class<?>...)
    Use ReflectHelper.tryGetMethod(Class, String, Class[]) instead, which returns a Try that can be used to handle the failure case more explicitly.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.ReflectHelper.getMethod(Class<C>, String, Class<?>...)
    Use ReflectHelper.tryGetMethod(Class, String, Class[]) instead, which returns a Try that can be used to handle the failure case more explicitly.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.ReflectHelper.invokeMethod(C, Method, Object...)
    Use ReflectHelper.tryInvokeMethod(Object, Method, Object...) instead, which returns a Try that can be used to handle the failure case more explicitly.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.StringFormatter.addonToCommaSeperated(String, Collection<String>)
    Method name has a spelling error. Use StringFormatter.addOnToCommaSeparated(String, Collection) instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.helpers.WorldNameChecker.checkFolder(String)
    Use WorldNameChecker.checkFolder(WorldKeyOrName) instead, which is more robust and supports namespaced keys.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.helpers.WorldNameChecker.hasWorldFolder(String)
    Use WorldNameChecker.hasWorldFolder(WorldKeyOrName) instead, which is more robust and supports namespaced keys.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.helpers.WorldNameChecker.isValidWorldFolder(String)
    Use WorldNameChecker.isValidWorldFolder(WorldKeyOrName) instead, which is more robust and supports namespaced keys.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.options.CloneWorldOptions.fromTo(LoadedMultiverseWorld, String)
    Cloning can be done from unloaded worlds as well. Use CloneWorldOptions.fromTo(MultiverseWorld, String) instead. To ensure you use the non-deprecated method, you need to downcast the loaded world to a MultiverseWorld before passing it in, for example: CloneWorldOptions.fromTo((MultiverseWorld) loadedWorld, newWorldName)
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.options.CloneWorldOptions.newWorldName()
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.options.CloneWorldOptions.world()
    Cloning can be done from unloaded worlds as well. Use CloneWorldOptions.fromWorld() instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.options.CreateWorldOptions.worldName()
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.options.ImportWorldOptions.worldName()
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.reasons.RemoveFailureReason.WORLD_NON_EXISTENT
    No longer in use as WorldManager.removeWorld(String) is now deprecated.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.WorldManager.getPotentialWorlds()
    Use PotentialWorldFinder.findPotentialWorlds() instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.WorldManager.loadWorld(String)
    Use WorldManager.loadWorld(LoadWorldOptions) instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.WorldManager.loadWorld(MultiverseWorld)
    Use WorldManager.loadWorld(LoadWorldOptions) instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.WorldManager.removeWorld(String)
    Get the MultiverseWorld yourself and use WorldManager.removeWorld(RemoveWorldOptions) instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.WorldManager.removeWorld(LoadedMultiverseWorld)
    Use WorldManager.removeWorld(RemoveWorldOptions) instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.WorldManager.removeWorld(MultiverseWorld)
    Use WorldManager.removeWorld(RemoveWorldOptions) instead.
  • Deprecated Classes
    Class
    Description
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.command.flags.RemovePlayerFlags
    The --remove-players flag is being removed in favor of a more flexible system that allows for specifying a destination for players to be teleported to when a world is unloaded. See RemovePlayerDestinationFlags.
  • Deprecated Methods
    Method
    Description
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.config.node.ConfigNode.Builder.onSetValue(BiConsumer<T, T>)
    Use ConfigNode.Builder.onLoadAndChange(NodeChangeCallback) instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.config.node.ValueNode.getSerializer()
    Use ValueNode.serialize(Object) and ValueNode.deserialize(Object) instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.config.node.ValueNode.onSetValue(T, T)
    Use ValueNode.onLoadAndChange(CommandSender, Object, Object) instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.destination.Destination.getDestinationInstance(String)
    Use Destination.getDestinationInstance(CommandSender, String) instead. This method will no longer be called by DestinationsProvider.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.event.world.MVWorldClonedEvent.getFromWorld()
    Cloning can be done from unloaded worlds as well. Use MVWorldClonedEvent.getSourceWorld() instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.module.MultiverseModule.registerEvents(Class<? extends Listener>)
    Use MultiverseModule.registerDynamicListeners(Class) with the new DynamicListener API.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.MultiverseCore.getApi()
    Use MultiverseCoreApi.get() directly.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.teleportation.AsyncSafetyTeleporterAction.teleport(Entity)
    Use AsyncSafetyTeleporterAction.teleportSingle(Entity) instead, as teleport of single entity may result in multiple teleports due to vehicle and passengers based on AsyncSafetyTeleporterAction.passengerMode(PassengerMode).
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.ReflectHelper.getClass(String)
    Use ReflectHelper.tryGetClass(String) instead, which returns a Try that can be used to handle the failure case more explicitly.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.ReflectHelper.getField(C, String)
    Use ReflectHelper.tryGetField(Class, String) instead, which returns a Try that can be used to handle the failure case more explicitly.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.ReflectHelper.getField(Class<C>, String)
    Use ReflectHelper.tryGetField(Class, String) instead, which returns a Try that can be used to handle the failure case more explicitly.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.ReflectHelper.getFieldValue(C, String, Class<V>)
    Use ReflectHelper.tryGetField(Class, String) then map to ReflectHelper.tryGetFieldValue(Object, Field, Class) instead, which returns a Try that can be used to handle the failure case more explicitly.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.ReflectHelper.getFieldValue(C, Field, Class<V>)
    Use ReflectHelper.tryGetFieldValue(Object, Field, Class) instead, which returns a Try that can be used to handle the failure case more explicitly.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.ReflectHelper.getMethod(C, String, Class<?>...)
    Use ReflectHelper.tryGetMethod(Class, String, Class[]) instead, which returns a Try that can be used to handle the failure case more explicitly.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.ReflectHelper.getMethod(Class<C>, String, Class<?>...)
    Use ReflectHelper.tryGetMethod(Class, String, Class[]) instead, which returns a Try that can be used to handle the failure case more explicitly.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.ReflectHelper.invokeMethod(C, Method, Object...)
    Use ReflectHelper.tryInvokeMethod(Object, Method, Object...) instead, which returns a Try that can be used to handle the failure case more explicitly.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.utils.StringFormatter.addonToCommaSeperated(String, Collection<String>)
    Method name has a spelling error. Use StringFormatter.addOnToCommaSeparated(String, Collection) instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.helpers.WorldNameChecker.checkFolder(String)
    Use WorldNameChecker.checkFolder(WorldKeyOrName) instead, which is more robust and supports namespaced keys.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.helpers.WorldNameChecker.hasWorldFolder(String)
    Use WorldNameChecker.hasWorldFolder(WorldKeyOrName) instead, which is more robust and supports namespaced keys.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.helpers.WorldNameChecker.isValidWorldFolder(String)
    Use WorldNameChecker.isValidWorldFolder(WorldKeyOrName) instead, which is more robust and supports namespaced keys.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.options.CloneWorldOptions.fromTo(LoadedMultiverseWorld, String)
    Cloning can be done from unloaded worlds as well. Use CloneWorldOptions.fromTo(MultiverseWorld, String) instead. To ensure you use the non-deprecated method, you need to downcast the loaded world to a MultiverseWorld before passing it in, for example: CloneWorldOptions.fromTo((MultiverseWorld) loadedWorld, newWorldName)
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.options.CloneWorldOptions.newWorldName()
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.options.CloneWorldOptions.world()
    Cloning can be done from unloaded worlds as well. Use CloneWorldOptions.fromWorld() instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.options.CreateWorldOptions.worldName()
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.options.ImportWorldOptions.worldName()
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.WorldManager.getPotentialWorlds()
    Use PotentialWorldFinder.findPotentialWorlds() instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.WorldManager.loadWorld(String)
    Use WorldManager.loadWorld(LoadWorldOptions) instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.WorldManager.loadWorld(MultiverseWorld)
    Use WorldManager.loadWorld(LoadWorldOptions) instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.WorldManager.removeWorld(String)
    Get the MultiverseWorld yourself and use WorldManager.removeWorld(RemoveWorldOptions) instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.WorldManager.removeWorld(LoadedMultiverseWorld)
    Use WorldManager.removeWorld(RemoveWorldOptions) instead.
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.WorldManager.removeWorld(MultiverseWorld)
    Use WorldManager.removeWorld(RemoveWorldOptions) instead.
  • Deprecated Enum Constants
    Enum Constant
    Description
    org.mvplugins.multiverse.core.world.reasons.RemoveFailureReason.WORLD_NON_EXISTENT
    No longer in use as WorldManager.removeWorld(String) is now deprecated.
